ARGUMENT IS HELD. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS "MOVE THIS COURT TO STAY THIS ACTION (INCLUDING VACATION OF THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE), OR, ALTERNATIVELY, MERELY VACATE THE JURY 12, 2021 TRIAL DATE." DEPARTMENT 302 IS NOT AUTHORIZED TO VACATE TRIAL DATES; SUCH MOTIONS MUST BE FILED IN DEPARTMENT 206. THE PENDING MOTION IN DEPARTMENT 302 IS THUS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR. JUDGE: RICHARD B. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tranfer Venue From The County Of San Francisco Superior Court To Sacramento County Superior Court; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Chase R. Morello
Off Calendar Dec-12-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Susan Kang Gordon, Esq. 510-400-6146 for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Chase Morello, Esq. 310-312-0772 of Meyers McConnell Reisz Sideman for Defendant Julie Ann Mesman through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT JULIE MESMAN's Motion To Tranfer Venue From The County Of San Francisco Superior Court To Sacramento County Superior Court.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.Defendant Julie Anne Mesman's motion to change venue is denied.

Defendant's motion to change venue under Code Civ. Proc. 397(a)'s mandatory grounds is denied. On a motion to change venue, the burden is on the moving party to show (1) that venue is proper in a different county than the one in which the lawsuit was filed and (2) that venue is improper in the current county under any applicable theory. (La Mirada Community Hospital v. Superior Court (1967) 249 Cal.App.2d 39, 42.) Although Defendant has established that Sacramento County would be a proper venue, it has failed to show that venue is improper in San Francisco County under any applicable theory. Under Code of Civ. Proc. 395(a) an action for personal injury is proper "in either the county where the injury occurs or the injury causing death occurs or the county where the defendants, or some of them reside." Although Defendant Mesman does not reside in San Francisco County, Defendant Uber Technologies' principal place of business is. (Kang Decl. ISO Opp., Ex. C.) Thus, venue is proper in San Francisco. (Code Civ. Proc. 395.5 ["A corporation may be sued in the county . . . the principal place of business of such corporation is situated"].)
Defendant's motion to change venue under Code Civ. Proc. 397(c)' discretionary grounds is denied. Under a 397(c) motion, "only convenience to third-party disinterested witnesses" may be considered. (Corfee, 202 Cal.App.2d at 478.) The convenience of the parties may not be considered. Thus, defendant's primary reliance on the purported inconvenience of the San Francisco forum to itself is misplaced. Defendant does not sufficiently identify or establish inconvenience of any other witness. Such a showing must be done "through affidavits which contain something more than generalities and conclusions." (Corfee, 202 Cal.App.2d at 477.) "[T]he supporting declarations must show (1) the name of each witness, (2) the expected testimony of each witness, and (3) facts showing why the attendance of said witnesses at trial will be inconvenient at trial and why the ends of justice would be served by a transfer." (Stute v. Burinda (1981) 123 Cal.App.Supp.3d 11, 17.)

Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in complaince with CRC 3.1312.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HEK)
